channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌍 Iran And Israel Step Back From The Brink | The War Zone
item.description: After a flurry of tit-for-tat strikes, Israel and Iran pulled back from entering into yet another all-out war. The post Iran And Israel Step Back From The Brink appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Airbus Unveils U145 Uncrewed Cargo Helicopter | The War Zone
item.description: The U145 transforms one of Europe’s most successful helicopters into an uncrewed aircraft optimized for risky cargo missions. The post Airbus Unveils U145 Uncrewed Cargo Helicopter appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms | Defense News
item.description: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.

item.title: 🏴 Is Skepticism Ukraine’s Foreign Policy Playbook? | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 📍 Franco-German-Spanish FCAS fighter program dead: Reports | Breaking Defense
item.description: The German Chancellor, Friedrich Merz, would have proposed that the two nations, along with Spain, which is also involved as a partner, continue to jointly develop the so-called combat cloud communication network.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet strikes, disables oil tanker in Gulf of Oman | Defense News
item.description: An F/A-18 Super Hornet assigned to the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the vessel's engineering and steering spaces.

item.title: 🌍 Show Us the Money | Foreign Policy
item.description: How satire, social media, and shame are forging Syria’s new public square.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Providing AI That’s Literate in Africa’s Languages | Foreign Policy
item.description: Chinese models have become the overwhelming choice for African developers.

item.title: 🏴 Germany and France drop joint fighter jet project | Defense News
item.description: Failure to reach an agreement underscores the struggles Europe has faced in rebuilding its military capacity after decades of underinvestment.

item.title: 🌏 State Department approves FMS agreements with New Zealand, South Korea | Breaking Defense
item.description: New Zealand could receive five Lockheed Martin MH-60R Seahawk helicopters as well as airborne low-frequency sonars.

item.title: 🏴 US must learn lessons from Ukraine, innovate faster and cheaper: Anduril president | Defense News
item.description: Anduril President Christian Brose discussed the need to develop cheaper weapons systems at scale to avoid quick depletion of exquisite munitions.

item.title: 🏴 Video Captures Rafale Fighter’s Drone Kill Over Baltic | The War Zone
item.description: What appears to be the first footage of an engagement of this kind in Europe underscores the growing challenge drones pose to NATO air policing. The post Video Captures Rafale Fighter’s Drone Kill Over Baltic appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Polish-Ukrainian startup develops radar to track elusive, low-flying drones | Defense News
item.description: The technology aims to find small, low-flying drones, including those connected to operators via long, fiber-optic command wires.

item.title: 🏴 Saab published in the Financial Times on Gulf support for Ukraine | Atlantic Council
item.description: On June 4, Forward Defense non-resident senior fellow Bilal Saab published an article in the Financial Times, discussing the benefit of a counter-drone defense partnership between the Gulf states and Ukraine. He argues this partnership would benefit Gulf states by integrating…

item.title: 🕵️ FCAS uncertainty and transatlantic upheaval: What to expect at the Berlin Air Show | Breaking Defense
item.description: Over 750 exhibitors from 37 countries are expected to display their equipment, say organizers, but the New Generation Fighter (NGF) aircraft, the centerpiece of FCAS, is not scheduled to make an appearance.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Making MAHA’s Favorite Drug | Foreign Policy
item.description: RFK fans and China hawks are on a collision course over peptides.

item.title: 🏴 French jet on NATO mission shoots down drone in Latvian airspace | Defense News
item.description: Military drones straying into the airspace of Russia’s neighbors have stoked concerns that Russia’s war in Ukraine is spilling over NATO’s borders.

item.title: 🏴 The United States is a reliable energy partner: The data proves it | Atlantic Council
item.description: The market for US LNG is global, liquid, flexible, and governed by commercial contracts, making it appealing to importers over supply from coercive exporters like Russia. The post The United States is a reliable energy partner: The data proves it appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 📍 The future of defense manufacturing will be distributed, autonomous and software-defined | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] How Roboze is helping transform additive manufacturing from a prototyping tool into production infrastructure for defense and critical industries.

item.title: 🌍 US approves $2B sale of Anduril counter-drone systems to Kuwait | Breaking Defense
item.description: The potential FMS announcement came just days Iran attacked Kuwait’s airport with missiles and drones, killing one person, damaging airport facilities and halting flights.

item.title: 🛡️ A Year Later: The Stakes of Ordering Military Personnel to Police American Streets | Just Security
item.description: One year since Trump sent the National Guard to LA, a new report warns military deployments for domestic policing produce escalation, disillusionment, and politicization. The post A Year Later: The Stakes of Ordering Military Personnel to Police American Streets appeared first…

item.title: 🕵️ AI ‘Regulation’ in the Chokepoint State | Just Security
item.description: President Trump’s new executive order on AI regulation rests on broad executive discretion and seeks to bypass judicial accountability. The post AI ‘Regulation’ in the Chokepoint State appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 The NBA’s Genocide Problem | Just Security
item.description: The NBA’s partnership with the United Arab Emirates is laundering the reputation of a regime that supports a militia responsible for ​committing genocide in Sudan. The post The NBA’s Genocide Problem appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Airbus unveils U145 autonomous helicopter drone for cargo supply ops | Breaking Defense
item.description: Other military roles for the drone cover armed scouting, surveillance, crewed-uncrewed teaming as well as acting as a “mothership” for air launched effects, according to the European prime.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 8, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated guide to major news and developments over the weekend. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs yesterday, in retaliation for a Hezbollah missile attack on northern Israel,…

item.title: 🌏 The U.S. Can’t Exclude China From Latin America | Foreign Policy
item.description: Rather than pushing Beijing out of the region, Washington should offer countries better strategic alternatives.

item.title: 🏛️ French Army has recruitment surplus but lacks equipment, deputy chief says | Defense News
item.description: “This year, we’re already slowing down recruitment because not only are we recruiting better, but we’re retaining people better," a service leader said.

item.title: 🌍 Palladyne AI partners with IAI to bring Israeli Harpy, Harop drones to US | Breaking Defense
item.description: Palladyne plans to put new AI swarming software on the battle-proven Israeli drones and compete for contracts like the Army’s Long-Range Precision Munition (LRPM), CEO Ben Wolff told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 📍 Collection: Just Security’s Coverage of Trump Administration Executive Actions | Just Security
item.description: Coverage of key developments, including in concise “What Just Happened” expert explainers, legal and policy analysis, and more. Check back frequently for updates. The post Collection: Just Security’s Coverage of Trump Administration Executive Actions appeared first on Just…

item.title: 📍 Thailand Says It Will Join UN-Backed Conciliation on Maritime Dispute With Cambodia | The Diplomat
item.description: Thai Foreign Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow said that Bangkok will cease all other bilateral talks while mediation is in progress.
